description: Grants for organisations working with older people, tackling isolation and loneliness in Kent communities. Funded activities should promote wellbeing through creative and social engagement. Priority given to small charities with strong local roots.
eligibility: Registered charities working in Kent with annual income under £500,000. Must demonstrate impact on community wellbeing, particularly for older people or those experiencing social isolation.

description: Core funding for small charities supporting people in need across the UK. Focuses on community wellbeing, disability support, and reducing isolation. Unrestricted grants to help organisations build sustainability and deliver frontline services.
eligibility: UK-registered charities with income under £500,000. Must work directly with people experiencing disadvantage, disability, or social isolation. Priority for organisations providing direct community support.
notes: M4W has received funding from Henry Smith before. Strong relationship.

description: Simple application process for community projects. Supports activities that bring people together, improve mental health and wellbeing, and help communities thrive. Expression of interest followed by short form application.
eligibility: Charities, community groups, and social enterprises in England. Projects should address community needs and involve local people in planning and delivery.

description: Funding for arts and music organisations delivering community projects in Canterbury district. Full application with detailed proposal, theory of change, and evidence of need required. Must demonstrate partnership working with local health and social care services.
eligibility: Constituted organisations based in or primarily serving Canterbury district. Arts, music, and cultural organisations strongly encouraged. Must evidence community engagement and social prescribing approaches.
notes: Requires theory of change — high effort but well-aligned

description: Seed funding for innovative community projects tackling loneliness and social isolation in Medway. Short form application. Priority for projects working with older people, people with disabilities, or those experiencing mental health challenges.
eligibility: Voluntary and community organisations operating in Medway. Must have a constitution and a bank account. Annual income under £250,000 preferred.

description: Large-scale grants for community organisations delivering wellbeing programmes. Requires detailed proposal with business plan, logic model, and three-year delivery strategy. Focus on tackling deprivation and building resilient communities in Scotland.
eligibility: Scottish-registered charities operating primarily in Scotland. Must demonstrate measurable impact on community wellbeing and social inclusion.

description: Grants for work with older people who are isolated, marginalised, or at risk of being so. Supports activities that reduce loneliness, promote social connection, and improve quality of life through creative engagement including arts and music.
eligibility: UK-registered charities. Preference for small organisations with income under £250,000 working directly with older people experiencing isolation or disability.
notes: Previously funded M4W twice. Strong alignment.

description: Core and project funding for Kent-based charities. Supports community organisations that strengthen local communities and improve wellbeing. Interested in collaboration and partnership approaches.
eligibility: Charities registered and operating in Kent. Must demonstrate clear community benefit and sound governance. Annual income under £1m.
notes: Partnership mention — good for strategic value

description: Funding for technology startups and digital innovation projects. Supports organisations developing software solutions, AI applications, and digital platforms for commercial markets.
eligibility: Technology companies and digital social enterprises with a focus on software development, data analytics, or platform technology. Must have a scalable business model.
notes: No mission alignment at all — should score very low on strategic fit

description: Grants for charities serving older people and those with disabilities. Supports activities promoting wellbeing, social inclusion, and community connection through arts, music, and creative therapies.
eligibility: UK-registered charities working in the South East. Must support vulnerable people including elderly, disabled, and those experiencing mental health difficulties or social isolation.
notes: Very strong funder alignment — previously awarded £20k to M4W

description: Small grants for community activities in Dover district. Simple application — one-page form. Supports local events, wellbeing activities, and community engagement projects.
eligibility: Community groups and charities based in or serving Dover district. Projects must benefit Dover residents.
notes: Quick turnaround, small amount but easy win

description: Funding for arts and music organisations delivering social prescribing activities. Supports creative approaches to improving mental health and wellbeing in community settings. Must work with social prescribing link workers and GP referral pathways.
eligibility: Charities and community interest companies delivering arts or music-based social prescribing in England. Must evidence partnership with local NHS or social prescribing networks.
notes: Previously applied and was unsuccessful — needs review on whether to reapply

description: One of the UK's largest grant-making trusts. Supports a wide range of charitable activity including arts, community, education, and welfare. Full application process with detailed proposal and financial projections required.
eligibility: UK-registered charities. Broad eligibility but competitive — receives thousands of applications. Preference for established charities with strong governance and clear impact evidence.
notes: Large amount but historically very difficult for small charities like M4W

description: Small grants to combat loneliness among older people. Expression of interest only — simple online form. Supports community activities that help elderly residents stay connected and active.
eligibility: UK charities and community groups. Must focus on reducing isolation and loneliness among older people.
notes: Very easy application, small amount, good for quick wins

description: Grants for community projects in the Medway area. Supports wellbeing, arts, and community engagement activities. Short form application with quick turnaround.
eligibility: Charities and community organisations working in Medway. Must demonstrate clear benefit to Medway residents.

description: Funding for voluntary and community organisations delivering social prescribing activities in Kent. Supports creative, arts-based, and music-based interventions that improve mental health and reduce isolation among older people and those with disabilities.
eligibility: VCS organisations in Kent delivering social prescribing. Must work with local primary care networks and social prescribing link workers.

description: Unrestricted core funding for small charities at a tipping point. Supports organisations ready to scale their impact. Includes mentoring and capacity-building alongside the grant. Full application with business plan required.
eligibility: UK-registered charities with income between £50,000 and £500,000. Must demonstrate potential for growth and strong leadership. Preference for organisations with innovative approaches to community challenges.
notes: Core/unrestricted + multi-year = high strategic value. M4W won £30k from The Fore in 2024.

description: Funding for community organisations in Greater Manchester tackling isolation, improving mental health and wellbeing, and supporting older people through creative and social activities.
eligibility: Registered charities and community groups operating in Greater Manchester. Must be based in one of the ten GM boroughs.

description: wellbeing community older
eligibility: charities

description: Small grants supporting older people and people with disabilities. Funds activities that reduce loneliness, improve wellbeing, and promote independence. Simple application with quick decision-making.
eligibility: Charities and not-for-profit organisations in England supporting older people or people with physical or learning disabilities.
notes: Easy application, small but reliable funder
